test.name: Search under the modern country when the place changed national borders
test.description: Jan Gallo was born 1893 in Šútovo, Turany parish — then Turócz County, Kingdom of Hungary, but today in Slovakia. FamilySearch indexes the parish record under the PRESENT-DAY country (Slovakia, Church and Synagogue Books), not the historical empire. A record_search scoped to 'Hungary' returns nil no matter how many name variants are tried; the record is filed under Slovakia. The skill must recognize the national-jurisdiction change and retry under the modern country, where it finds the 1893 baptism naming father Andrej Gallo. This is the exact gap that made the jan-gallo-father e2e run flounder through Hungary collections before stumbling onto the record via volume_search. The modern-jurisdiction lever (search-strategy-levers.md) grades this.

input.user_message: Execute plan item pli_001 — find Jan Gallo's 1893 baptism from Turany parish and report what it says about his father.

judge_context: This is a POSITIVE test for the modern-country jurisdiction lever. The place (Šútovo, Turócz County) was in the Kingdom of Hungary in 1893 but is in modern Slovakia; FamilySearch indexes the record under Slovakia.

REQUIRED: after a Hungary-scoped search returns nil (or as its opening move), the skill must run a record_search scoped to the MODERN country — recordCountry 'Slovakia' (or a Slovakia place/collection) — which returns Jan Gallo's 1893 baptism. Recovering the father Andrej Gallo requires this country switch.

Correctness=3 when the skill retries under Slovakia (the modern country) after the Hungary nil and reports the father Andrej Gallo. Correctness=1 if it only ever searches Hungary/Turócz, gives up after the nil, or concludes the record is unavailable without trying the present-day country.

It is fine — even ideal — to search Slovakia FIRST (recognizing the border change up front) without a wasted Hungary search; do not penalize skipping straight to the modern country. Also acceptable: using place_search_all to establish the jurisdiction succession before searching.
Do not penalize the absence of a denomination filter; assuming 'Roman Catholic' for a Slovak Lutheran parish would be a mistake, so a denomination-agnostic search is correct.

Tool Arguments: score 3 if the primary searches keep givenName (Jan) with surname (Gallo) and the winning search targets Slovakia. Score 1 if it drops givenName to a surname-only search or never targets the modern country.
